name: Khalid ibn Amr
title: Son of Al-Saydawi
shortDescription: The young son of Amr ibn Khalid al-Saydawi who went to the battlefield following his father's martyrdom and fought with utmost bravery.
fullStory:
Khalid ibn Amr al-Saydawi was the son of Amr ibn Khalid (one of the great martyrs of Karbala). He was raised in a brave family devoted to the Ahl al-Bayt in Kufa.

When his father Amr ibn Khalid decided to secretly leave Kufa to assist Imam Hussain (AS), Khalid gladly accompanied him. Along with a few others (including Mujammi al-A'idhi and Nafi ibn Hilal), they joined the Imam's caravan mid-journey.

On the day of Ashura, his father Amr ibn Khalid sought permission from the Imam, went to the battlefield, and was martyred. With his father's martyrdom, Khalid's blood boiled. He immediately went to the Imam and asked for permission to fight.

Despite his young age, Khalid drew his sword and, counting the moments until martyrdom, struck the heart of the enemy army. He fought heroically until he was surrounded by Umar Sa'd's soldiers and attained the great honor of martyrdom, joining his father in Paradise.
quoteArabic: صَبْراً عَلى المَوْتِ بَنِي قَحْطانَ، كَيْما تَكُونُوا فِي رِضى الرَّحْمانِ...
quote: (In his war poetry:) O tribe of Qahtan! Be patient upon death so that you may gain the pleasure of the Merciful God...
source: Manaqib Al Abi Talib, Ibn Shahr Ashub
